title: Rubroshorea smithiana
intro: Rubroshorea smithiana is a species of plant in the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the Mahua tree, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and the risk of infections. Bioactive constituents of Rubroshorea smithiana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rubroshorea smithiana is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin diseases, and respiratory issues.

This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is a member of the order Malpighiales. Within the family Hypericaceae, Rubroshorea smithiana belongs to the tribe Hypericeae, a group of flowering plants characterized by their five-petaled flowers.
